subject: Reg CB Counter Taobao Weekly Report 20250704
deck_filename: Counter Taobao Weekly Update - Week ending 4 Jul.pdf

content.answer:
Hi All,

Please find the weekly Counter Taobao project update as of 30th June below.

Key Highlights

   -

   Fashion:
   -

      ADO/ADG Target Overachieve: ADO 40K (106% RR), ADG 433K (104% RR)
      driven by 1) newly acquired sellers with strong performance in TH (Top
      seller grew to 825 ADO in M3 of onboarding) & VN, 2) existing sellers
      growth in PH supported by platform reinvestment (PC2 reduced to -5%).
      -

      SKU Target Underachieve:  SKU# at 696k (RR at 22%)  due to delay in
      SKU onboarding via Lever 3 as system trials are ongoing and
existing manual
      onboarding process (i.e. shop creation and SKU listing) needs to be
      improved prior mass pitching in July.
      -

      Program PC2 -1.90% driven by re-investment in program sellers from
      MY(-2%) TH (-2%) and PH (-5%)
      -

   Furniture:
   -

      ADO/ADG Target Overachieve: ADO 1.9K (103% RR), ADG $84K (99% RR)
      driven by TH with platform investment on high ABS items.
      -

      SKU Target Underachieve: SKU# at 271k (RR at 93%)  due to delay in
      SKU onboarding via Lever 3 as system trials are ongoing and
existing manual
      onboarding process (i.e. shop creation and SKU listing) needs to be
      improved prior mass pitching in July.
      -

      Program PC2: 10.31% due to increase in take-rate in SG/MY following
      commission fee changes. WIP to discuss reinvestment plans.
      -

   F&B & Beauty: Pitching commenced in June’25. Seller program tracking and
   incentive disbursement will start from Jul’25


Key Initiatives

   -

   Lever 1 - New Seller Acquisition: By Jun '25, 168 TB sellers have
   accepted to join SHP; of which 144 TB sellers have opened shops on SHP (95
   Fashion, 42 Furniture, 3 F&B, 4 Beauty).  94 sellers are shortlisted to
   join our incentive program; will continue to repitch rejected sellers
   through ERP solutions.
   -

   Lever 2 - SKU Sourcing By Tr

content.question:
Thanks.



Slide 3

   - Are the programs standardized across the markets?

Especially for
   Fashion and Beauty?
So it can be pitched in an easy way?
- For beauty, can we get PH in as well?
Probably no downside to get them?

Slide 4

   - For new sellers, are our targets too low?

We need to aim for higher
   impact rom this initiative?

As compared to just setting low targets and
   then “achieving” them?

- When we acquire a new Taobao seller, do we get them started for all
   markets?
Or is it only for 1 market?

Slide 5

   - For new seller, we can get them started for PH market as well?

- For new sellers, they seem to be doing much better in TH as compared
   to the other markets, is there a reason why?

Slide 6-7

   - Impact is low overall. New sellers does not seem to be driving much,
   from seller numbers to actual sales



Slide 12

   - Can we do ads on the JST?

Will they give us the list of sellers to
   work with?
If not how hard is it to scrap seller details from TB?
- For the seller UX issues, are they still committed to fix it?
- Where did we land on ERP pricing reflecting true TB pricing?

How will
   we solve for that?

Slide 14

   - I am not quite sure about this exercise in general and what we are
   trying to solve
   - Are we trying to ensure that onboarded TB sellers lower their prices
   on Shopee?
Because of the ERP situation?
